failsafes: backup float switch behind/above primary optical sensor
knownIssues: Kalkwasser dosing through the unit causes premature pump wear, not covered under warranty (per BRS product page)
Optical sensor accumulates calcium/salt deposits and needs periodic cleaning with vinegar or citric-acid solution
Loud during active fill cycles per BRS customer reviews
Magnetic sensor mount can shift position over time
Float-switch only — no optical sensor on this nano model

failsafes: QST real-time fail-safe cutoff if sensor moves/fails
siphon break mounted above water level
knownIssues: No secondary/backup sensor — Reef2Reef owners report the single optical sensor sticking, causing full-reservoir dumps
Overfilling reported specifically overnight/in the dark
Siphon break normally drips while the pump runs — frequently mistaken for a leak (BRS Q&A)

failsafes: alarm if a fill cycle runs 6x longer than the learned baseline
knownIssues: Multiple Reef2Reef owners report pump/sensor failures at 12-14 months — just past the 1-year warranty ($70-195 out-of-pocket)
Documented overfill: an entire ~20-gal reservoir pumped into a 50-gal tank overnight, dropping salinity to 1.018
Slow customer-service turnaround and limited replacement-part availability reported

failsafes: backup optical sensor behind the primary
mechanical float valve as final overflow backup
IQ-Fill run-time cutoff at 2x the learned normal fill
atos.controllerTieIn: standalone; extra notifications/logging when paired with an Apex
knownIssues: Sensor plugs not fully seated in the FMM module (needs a firm click) cause stuck-open/closed readings — a frequent troubleshooting thread topic
Widespread PMUP pump impeller failure reports — the PMUP's BRS review rating sits around ~2.5/5 stars
Sparse documentation on how the fill logic actually works

failsafes: second optical sensor backs up the primary
knownIssues: Owners report random small top-offs even with a clean sensor, gradually diluting the tank
Snails/hermit crabs climbing the optical sensor is a commonly cited false-trigger
Direct sunlight on the sensor reported to cause false triggering

atos.sensorType: optical, primary + backup in one probe
failsafes: backup sensor 1 inch above the primary in the same probe
pump-performance monitor shuts down a stalled/dry pump
knownIssues: Reef2Reef owners report the combined sensor housing misreading level, causing 0.5-0.6 gal single-shot overfills
Pump reported to run dry without shutting off when the reservoir emptied — no app notification sent
Some owners report an audible whine, no quieter than the float systems it replaces

categoryNotes: Optical sensors are preferred over plain float switches because they have no moving parts to stick or jam from salt creep and calcium build-up — the classic float-switch failure mode.

BRS evaporation guideline: most owners refill a 5-gallon reservoir about weekly on tanks up to ~50-100 gallons; ~10-gallon weekly for systems up to ~200 gallons. Owner reports put it around 1-3% of system volume per day, higher in winter.

The classic ATO failure is a stuck-on sensor: overfill lowers salinity and can flood the room. Failsafe stacking (dual sensors, run-time cutoffs, mechanical float valves) exists specifically to catch this.
